descriptions: The United Issarak Front was a coalition of Khmer Issarak groups, which later evolved into the Khmer Rouge.
edges.evidence_quote: On 17 April 1950, the first nationwide congress of the Khmer Issarak groups convened, and the United Issarak Front was established.

descriptions: The Khmer Rouge and the Khmer People's National Liberation Front were both members of the Coalition Government of Democratic Kampuchea.
edges.evidence_quote: China, the United States and the ASEAN countries sponsored the creation and the military operations of a Cambodian government in exile, known as the Coalition Government of Democratic Kampuchea, which included the Khmer Rouge, the republican Khmer People's National Liberation Front and the royalist Funcinpec Party.

descriptions: Members of both gangs were involved in a shooting together, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: In October 2017, members of the Killer Beez and the 36 (Three Six) gangs were involved in a shooting, where three males in Ōtara were shot in a drive-by shooting.

Original Red Devils Motorcycle Club.description: Canadian outlaw motorcycle club founded in Hamilton, Ontario in 1948. It operated three chapters in Ontario and was known for staying small and maintaining good relationships with other clubs, including the Hells Angels. The club patched over to Bacchus Motorcycle Club in 2014.

descriptions: Bacchus patched over the Original Red Devils, absorbing their chapters.
edges.evidence_quote: On 8 November 2014, Bacchus MC patched over their closest ally, the Original Red Devils Motorcycle Club , to create the first Ontario expansion of the club absorbing the Red Devils three Ontario chapters in Hamilton , Chatham-Kent and Sudbury .

descriptions: Hells Angels and Red Devils jointly attacked an Outlaws clubhouse.
edges.evidence_quote: On October 4, 2009, several Hells Angels and allied Red Devils performed a raid on an Outlaws clubhouse in Kortrijk.

descriptions: Red Devils is a smaller motorcycle gang allied with Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: The Hells Angels are allied with numerous smaller motorcycle gangs – such as the ... Red Devils

descriptions: Hells Angels aligned with Red Devils.
edges.evidence_quote: The Australian Hells Angels have aligned themselves with the Coffin Cheaters , Immortals, Red Devils , Satans Soldiers, Vikings and the Prisoners of War

descriptions: Hells Angels formed alliances with Red Devils.
edges.evidence_quote: Additionally, the club has also formed alliances with various street gangs, including the Independent Soldiers, the Red Scorpions, the United Nations and the White Boy Posse, and smaller motorcycle gangs, such as Bacchus, the Gate Keepers and the Red Devils.

descriptions: The Original Red Devils merged into Bacchus MC by patching over their chapters.
edges.evidence_quote: They would end up patching over to long-time ally Bacchus Motorcycle Club in late 2014, with their three chapters Hamilton , Chatham-Kent , and Sudbury joining Bacchus MC.

descriptions: The Original Red Devils MC merged into Bacchus in 2015.
edges.evidence_quote: This club patched over to the East Coast based Bacchus in 2015.

descriptions: The Original Red Devils have good relationships with Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: All of this has led to the Red Devils being "comfortable in their own skin," and have good relationships with other clubs, including the Hells Angels.

descriptions: Red Devils participated in a raid on Outlaws clubhouse alongside Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: On October 4, 2009, several Hells Angels and allied Red Devils performed a raid on an Outlaws clubhouse in Kortrijk.

Red Army (football firm).description: The Red Army is a hooligan firm associated with English football club Manchester United. Founded in the 1970s, it became one of the largest firms in British football, known for causing mayhem at away matches. The firm includes criminal sub-divisions involved in drug smuggling, armed robberies, and theft. Its activities have declined since the late 1980s.

edges.evidence_quote: there are also sub-divisions of the firm known as the Young Munichs, the Inter City Jibbers (ICJ), the M58 Firm and the Moston Rats.

Black Jackets.description: Primarily ethnically Turkish criminal gang active in Germany, the Netherlands, and Denmark. Formed in 1985 in Heidenheim an der Brenz by immigrants from Turkey, Italy, and former Yugoslavia. Engages in arms trafficking, drug trafficking, robbery, extortion, arson, prostitution, burglary, and vandalism. Claims to be a brotherhood rather than an outlaw motorcycle club or criminal organization.

Sabaot Land Defence Force.description: The Sabaot Land Defence Force (SLDF) was a guerrilla militia operating in the Mount Elgon District of Kenya from 2005 to 2008. It drew members from the Sabaot people and was accused of killing over 600 people, committing atrocities such as murder, torture, and rape, and displacing more than 66,000 people. The group was led by Wycliffe Matakwei Komon and had a clear organizational structure with political, spiritual, and military wings. It was funded through unofficial taxation of local residents.

Mallardo clan.description: The Mallardo clan is a Camorra crime clan based in Giugliano, Campania, Italy, founded in the late 1970s by Francesco Mallardo. It is a key member of the Secondigliano Alliance, considered the most powerful active Camorra group. The clan is involved in racketeering, smuggling, extortion, and drug trafficking, and has dominated the Neapolitan underworld since the 1990s. In 2011, Italian police seized assets worth €600 million from the clan.

descriptions: Mallardo clan is one of the clans holding real power within the Camorra.
edges.evidence_quote: According to Misso, the real power of the organization is now in the hands of the Licciardi clan, Mallardo clan, Moccia clan and Contini clan.

edges.evidence_quote: the Mallardo clan, from Giugliano in Campania, named after Francesco Mallardo, among others.

descriptions: The Mallardo are a Camorra clan that established territorial control in the 1970s.
edges.evidence_quote: Nell'area di Giugliano si affermarono i Mallardo con capacità di controllo territoriale che risalgono proprio a questo decennio.

Finks Motorcycle Club.description: Australian outlaw motorcycle club formed in Sydney in 1969. Named after a character from The Wizard of Id cartoon, the club uses a jester logo. It has chapters in multiple Australian states and internationally. The club has been involved in rivalries and legal restrictions, with many members switching to the Mongols Motorcycle Club in 2013.

descriptions: Bandidos consider Finks as rivals in Australia.
edges.evidence_quote: while their rivals include the Comancheros, Finks, Gypsy Jokers, Hells Angels, Mongols, Notorious, Rebels and Red Devils

descriptions: A Finks member shot a Bandidos member in 2012, leading to conviction.
edges.evidence_quote: Mark James Graham, a Finks member who would later patch-over to the Mongols , was convicted of attempted murder and was sentenced to twelve years and three months in prison in November 2014.

descriptions: Bandidos and Finks engaged in a mass brawl.

edges.evidence_quote: A principle motivation behind the activities of Featherstone's faction appeared to be the gang's animosity towards Troy Forniciari, the self-appointed leader of the Illawarra chapter of the Finks Motorcycle Club.

descriptions: Finks members visited a former Comanchero member in hospital.
edges.evidence_quote: While Vince was in hospital, he was visited by friends who were Finks members.

descriptions: Mongols absorbed most Finks members in a patch-over in 2013.
edges.evidence_quote: In October 2013, a deal was brokered to 'patch over' the Finks ahead of a proposed ban on the Finks under anti-gang legislation in Queensland. An estimated 90% of the Finks' members joined the Mongols.

Hawkhurst Gang.description: The Hawkhurst Gang was a notorious criminal organization involved in smuggling throughout south-east England from 1735 until 1749. Based in Hawkhurst, Kent, they extended their influence along the South coast and successfully raided the Custom House in Poole. After a defeat in battle with the Goudhurst militia in 1747, leaders Arthur Gray and Thomas Kingsmill were executed in 1748 and 1749, respectively.

descriptions: Los Fantasmas is a violent cell of the Cártel del Noreste.

descriptions: Revolutionary Justice Organisation is possibly a front group sponsored by Hezbollah.
edges.evidence_quote: Hezbollah ... is the possible sponsor of a number of lesser-known militant groups, some of which may be little more than fronts for Hezbollah itself, including ... the Revolutionary Justice Organisation

Forty-Two Gang.description: The Forty-Two Gang was a teenage street gang in Chicago active from 1925 to 1934, founded by Italian-American youths in the 'Patch' and 'Little Hell' neighborhoods. It served as a recruitment pool for the Chicago Outfit, producing future mobsters like Sam Giancana. The gang engaged in vandalism, theft, burglary, and armed robbery, and was known for extreme violence. It disbanded after heavy losses from rival wars and arrests.

descriptions: The Forty-Two Gang is listed as an ally of the Chicago Outfit in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Forty-Two Gang

descriptions: The Chicago Outfit killed a Forty-Two Gang leader who interfered with their operations.
edges.evidence_quote: the Outfit eliminated him in 1938 due to his interference in their operations.

descriptions: The Chicago Outfit hired Forty-Two Gang members for occasional jobs.
edges.evidence_quote: The Outfit would occasionally hire gang members as beer runners or truck drivers

descriptions: The Forty-Two Gang supplied future members to the Chicago Outfit.
edges.evidence_quote: the Forty-Two Gang served as a 'farm team' for future members of the Chicago Outfit.
